steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content: For a parabola in the form $f(x)=a(x-h)^2+k$ with $a>0$, the minimum value is displayed by the constant $k$.

steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Rewrite the quadratic by completing the square.
$$x^2-8x-240=(x-4)^2-16-240$$
$$x^2-8x-240=(x-4)^2-256$$

steps.title: Identify the matching form
steps.content: The equivalent form that displays the minimum value as a constant is $f(x)=(x-4)^2+(-256)$. The answer is B.
